ads.title: Gate Of Doom 1990 Full Size Stand Up Arcade Game
ads.description: Gate of Doom, known in Japan as Dark Seal (ダークシール), is a hack 'n slash game developed by Data East and released in 1990 for arcades. Gate of Doom is a hack 'n slash game which uses a top-down, isometric perspective. Players can choose between four different characters, each with their own characteristics and abilities. The player can move and attack in eight directions, as well as unleash a powerful spell once its accumulated enough magic power. Much like in Karnov, the player can pick between available spells by tapping the joystick left and right. Levels are filled with enemies and traps, as well as treasure chests which the player can open to obtain various items and power-ups which give passive boosts to the character.

ads.author: Shane Michael
ads.title: Gottlieb Corral Pinball Machine
ads.description: This is a GREAT deal for this classic! I won't be lowering the price any further, though i'm still open to reasonable offers. For any less, i'll just find a spot for it in the house where my wife won't notice and keep playing it. I just have too many projects and not enough space, so i'm going to start pricing these a bit lower as i list them. My loss, your gain, all that jazz. **I GUARANTEE full function for 30 days if I deliver and set up.** This is a great early-60's Gottlieb EM single-player "wedgehead" pinball machine. I wish I could keep it. I love the 60's wedgies. The artwork is a lovely western theme featuring cowgals and horses. It has a rather charming and family friendly appeal to it. The object of this game is to spot all ten numbers, with a variety of ways to achieve this: rollovers, hitting the roto target, or with the wagon wheel in the middle, which changes numbers every time a 1-point switch is triggered. Add in the interesting flipper/outlane arrangement, and it makes for a very fun and very challenging game of pinball. Nudging is key. If you don't know how nudging works, this is a great machine to learn on. It has been freshly serviced and shopped out, and it came together beautifully. The pictures give a good idea of the condition. Feel free to ask for more pictures, details, or info on my extensive refurb process. I included a picture of the original condition for reference. The only issues are that the backglass paint is lifting in areas (it presents well and it's holding together ok; it should stay intact if you set the machine up in a climate controlled area) and some flaky light sockets causing the occasional flickering lamp. This is just something that happens with age. I clean them as best i can, but it would be far too expensive and labor intensive to replace them all. That would be part of a total restoration. Everything works as it should and it will provide plenty of fun for anyone that enjoys a good old fashioned game of pinball. Overall, it's in better shape than most 65 year-olds i know. Trades are unlikely but I won't be offended if you offer. Delivery available for a fee. Located in Leslie, MI 49251 ***Shameless Self Promotion*** I can also shop, refurb, or restore your pin. I focus on EMs mid-50's thru late 70's but do quite a bit with earlier SS pins as well. I have dozens of examples of my work for reference. I also generally have quite a few project pins on hand (see below), which i can service to whatever level you desire in a pre-sale arrangement. The minimum work I will do on those is getting them fully functional, tested, and shopped. Prices range from $600-$1300, except HH and Pinbot, which will be ~$3200. Titles currently in stock: Bally Bow and Arrow Playmatic Speakeasy Gottlieb Soccer Gottlieb Jack in the Box Gottlieb Miss Annabelle Gottlieb Haunted House (might keep) Williams Jubilee Williams World Cup (1978 early SS) Williams PinBot (already fully restored) I (generally) do not sell project machines. I do buy project machines. If you read all that, you may have too much free time. Thanks!

ads.author: Tim Timmins
ads.title: Dig Dug Video Arcade Game Machine ORIGINAL WORKING Atari Namco
ads.description: Here's an original Dig Dug arcade game - this is the REAL DEAL from back in the day! The game is running all original hardware and works fully, passing all self-tests and retaining all scores when turned off as it should. All buttons, switches, lights (yes, even the coin door lights) work. The cabinet has had wear around the edges and one area that was needed bondo as well. These areas were sanded, sealed and repainted. The t-molding was removed for this process, which was then cleaned and reinstalled. The black player-facing areas were all repainted and look perfect. The original sideart is all present and is in very good shape with no major gouges or defects. The marquee is original and in good shape. The original glass bezel artwork was stripped (flaking badly) and new repro reverse-printed artwork applied - looks a very nice-a. The control panel overlay is original and in very good shape, with only one small, tasteful cigarette burn in the upper right and some scuffing on the front. The panel itself was stripped of parts and the overlay cleaned with Awesome! and Magic Eraser to make those pinks and yellows really pop! The buttons and joystick were disassembled and cleaned. The bolts holding the joystick were also removed and burnished. The original coin door was disassembled, stripped and refinished and looks perfect! It can take quarters if you wish, as the coin box is present, but no locks are included. The original monitor has had the voltage properly adjusted and looks very good. Best thing is - IT'S BURN-FREE, a rarity for this title. Lastly, the original back door is included, with the instruction sheet AND the manual and schematics package. This is one of my favs - make it yours today! Cash and carry in Plano. Play before you pay. First floor, no stairs and I can help you load! :D
